revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yohashira_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
'''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]]にある[[神社]]。神は「柱」と数え、天之御中主神、高皇産霊神、神皇産霊神、天照大神の4柱が祀られていることから四柱神社という。願いごと結びの神として[[全国]]各地から[[信仰]]が篤い。
'''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]]にある[[神社]]。神は「柱」と数え、天之御中主神、高皇産霊神、神皇産霊神、天照大神の4柱が祀られていることから四柱神社という。願いごと結びの神として[[全国]]各地から[[信仰]]が篤い。 {{神道}}

[[画像:Yohashira jinja.jpg|thumb|280px|四柱神社]] {{神道}} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]]にある[[神社]]。神は「柱」と数え、天之御中主神、高皇産霊神、神皇産霊神、天照大神の4柱が祀られていることから四柱神社という。地元では、「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれている。願いごと結びの神として[[全国]]各地から[[信仰]]が篤い。

{{神社| 名称=四柱神社| 画像=[[画像:Yohashira jinja.jpg|250px|四柱神社]]| 所在地=〒390-0874
長野県松本市大手3-3-20| 祭神=天之御中主神
高皇産霊神
神皇産霊神
天照大神| 社格=村社・別表神社| 創建=明治12年（1879年）| 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭）}} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれている。 [[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀ることから四柱神社という。よく「しはしらじんじゃ」と間違えて呼ばれる。願いごと結びの神として[[全国]]各地から[[信仰]]が篤い。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、大正13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。 == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]] 徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] [[Category:松本市|よはしらしんしや]] [[Category:長野県の神社|よはしらしんしや]] [[Category:村社|よはしらしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|よはしらしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

天照大神| 社格=村社・別表神社| 創建=明治12年（1879年）| 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭）}} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。誤読ではあるが「しはしら神社」とも呼ばれている。 == 概要 == 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀ることによる。願いごと結びの神として周辺地域からの信仰が篤い。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列。 「[[バナナマンの決断までのカウントダウン|バナナマンの決断は金曜日！]]」（フジテレビ系、2014年12月5日放送）および「[[やりすぎ都市伝説]]」（テレビ東京）にて、[[ゲッターズ飯田]]により[[パワースポット]]として紹介された。 == 交通アクセス == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|270px|境内風景]] *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀ることによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列。 == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]]バス松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = {{ill|File:Yohashira jinja800.jpg|en|Yohashira Shrine}}
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|en|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|en|天照大神}} | rank = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = Japan Nagano | label_position = bottom }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|en|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō Festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|en|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|en|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|en|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|en|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|en|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|en|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|en|松本市時計博物館}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀ることによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列。 == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|en|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|en|天照大神}} | rank = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|en|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō Festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|en|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|en|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|en|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|en|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|en|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|en|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|en|松本市時計博物館}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]]

{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|en|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|en|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|en|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|en|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|en|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|en|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|en|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation revision.comment: 祭神等に関する出典の追加
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=四柱神社 | 画像=[[画像:Looking through the torii at Yohashira-jinja, Matsumoto, 2016.jpg|250px|四柱神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | 所在地=〒390-0874
[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手3-3-20 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14|緯度秒 = 5.9 | 経度度 =137 |経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 13.4 | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]
[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。x == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | 所在地=〒390-0874
[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手3-3-20 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14|緯度秒 = 5.9 | 経度度 =137 |経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 13.4 | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]
[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。 == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。 この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=松本市史　第二巻　歴史編Ⅲ　 |publisher=松本市 |date= |page=247 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=新松本繁盛記　 |publisher=新信州社 |date= |page=216 |isbn=}} == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。境内には「明治天皇行在所旧址」の碑が残る。 この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=松本市史　第二巻　歴史編Ⅲ　 |publisher=松本市 |date= |page=247 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=新松本繁盛記　 |publisher=新信州社 |date= |page=216 |isbn=}} == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停下車してすぐ == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=四柱神社 | 画像=[[画像:Looking through the torii at Yohashira-jinja, Matsumoto, 2016.jpg|250px|四柱神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | 所在地=〒390-0874
[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手3-3-20 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14|緯度秒 = 5.9 | 経度度 =137 |経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 13.4 | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]
[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。境内には「明治天皇行在所旧址」の碑が残る。 この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=松本市史　第二巻　歴史編Ⅲ　 |publisher=松本市 |date= |page=247 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=新松本繁盛記　 |publisher=新信州社 |date= |page=216 |isbn=}} == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停（松本駅・[[松本バスターミナル|バスターミナル]]発）または本町バス停（松本駅行き）下車。 == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=四柱神社 | 画像=[[画像:Looking through the torii at Yohashira-jinja, Matsumoto, 2016.jpg|250px|四柱神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | 所在地=〒390-0874
[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手3-3-20 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14|緯度秒 = 5.9 | 経度度 =137 |経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 13.4 | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]
[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。境内には「明治天皇行在所旧址」の碑が残る。 この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=松本市史　第二巻　歴史編Ⅲ　 |publisher=松本市 |date= |page=247 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=新松本繁盛記　 |publisher=新信州社 |date= |page=216 |isbn=}} == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停（松本駅・[[松本バスターミナル|バスターミナル]]発）または本町バス停（松本駅行き）下車。 == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874

Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}}== See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yohashira Shrine | image = Yohashira jinja800.jpg | location = 〒390-0874
Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社[[ja:四柱神社]], Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|12=draft}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|12=draft}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行|12=draft}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=draft}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{Myōken Faith}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:bgoodbye $1]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yohashira Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yohashira Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:四柱神社]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[en:Yohashira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}}

It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=draft|12=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|12=draft|12=draft}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行|12=draft|12=draft}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] {{citation needed|Yohashira Shrine}} {{Myōken Faith}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yohashira Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[en:Yohashira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=四柱神社 | 画像=[[画像:Looking through the torii at Yohashira-jinja, Matsumoto, 2016.jpg|250px|四柱神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | 所在地=〒390-0874
[[長野県]][[松本市]]大手3-3-20 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14|緯度秒 = 5.9 | 経度度 =137 |経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 13.4 | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]
[[高皇産霊神]]
[[神皇産霊神]]
[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]） | 例祭=[[10月2日]]（神道祭） | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''四柱神社'''（よはしらじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[大手 (松本市)|大手]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。地元では、10月の例祭「神道祭」から「しんとう（神道）さん」と呼ばれる。また地元では四柱を「しはしら」と誤読される傾向にある。 == 概要 == [[画像:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|境内風景]] 名称は[[天之御中主神]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[天照大神]]を祭神とし、4柱の神を祀る{{Cite book|和書 |title=全国神社名鑑 上巻 |date= |year=1977 |publisher=全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター |page=263 |doi=10.11501/12262001}}ことによる。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[筑摩県]]松本に設置された神道[[中教院]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1997 |publisher=東京堂出版 |page=353 |isbn=4-490-10474-X}}で、明治7年（[[1874年]]）から四柱の神を祀ったことに始まる。明治12年（[[1879年]]）に現在地に社殿を造営し、[[村社]]の四柱神社として[[遷座]]した。明治21年（[[1888年]]）の松本大火で社殿を焼失し、[[大正]]13年（[[1924年]]）に再建した。[[昭和]]34年（[[1959年]]）、[[別表神社]]に加列{{Cite book|和書 |title=神社新報選 |year=1961 |publisher=神社新報社 |page=156 |doi=10.11501/3451839 |edition=昭和36年版}}。 ==天皇行幸== [[明治13年]]（[[1880年]]）6月24日、25日　明治天皇が松本市内を巡幸。24日午後には新築間もない神道事務分局の行在所に到着。境内には「明治天皇行在所旧址」の碑が残る。 この折、神社境内に渡る御幸橋が石造りで建造された。 三の丸の堀に架けられた太鼓橋である。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=松本市史　第二巻　歴史編Ⅲ　 |publisher=松本市 |date= |page=247 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=新松本繁盛記　 |publisher=新信州社 |date= |page=216 |isbn=}} == 交通アクセス == *[[JR]][[松本駅]]から徒歩10分 *[[アルピコ交通]][[松本電鉄バス|（通称・松本電鉄バス）]]松本城経由で大名町バス停（松本駅・[[松本バスターミナル|バスターミナル]]発）または本町バス停（松本駅行き）下車。 == 周辺情報 == *[[松本城]] *[[縄手通り商店街]] *[[八十二銀行]]松本営業部 *[[松本市時計博物館]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} *[http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ 四柱神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よはしらしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:国家神道]] [[Category:別表神社]]

Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Yohashira Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yohashira Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[en:Yohashira Shrine]]

{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en||ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR|ja|JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|ja|松本城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachijūni Bank|ja|八十二銀行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yohashira Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[en:Yohashira Shrine]]

[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|qq=}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|qq=}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|qq=}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|qq=}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto City]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[en:Yohashira Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden}} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|qq=}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|qq=}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|qq=}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|qq=}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}}
