It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|qq=|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|qq=|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|qq=|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}}

In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|qq=|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|qq=|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Yohashira Shrine]] [[sn:Yohashira Shrine]] [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}}

In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|qq=|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|qq=|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}}

Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden| location = {{ill|Ōte|WD=Q11435537}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.234972|N|137.970389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|1=Ōte|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435537}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden| location = {{ill|Ōte|WD=Q11435537}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.234972|N|137.970389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|1=Ōte|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435537}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:四柱神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11420029}}

Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden| location = {{ill|Ōte|WD=Q11435537}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.234972|N|137.970389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|1=Ōte|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435537}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:四柱神社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden| location = {{ill|Ōte|WD=Q11435537}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.234972|N|137.970389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|1=Ōte|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435537}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]] on the [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * [[:en:Matsumoto Castle|Matsumoto Castle]] * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * [[:en:Hachijuni Bank|Hachijūni Bank]] Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四柱神社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Daikyōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}} on the {{ill|Japan Railways Group|en|Japan Railways Group|WD=Q864495|lt=JR}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|WD=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto Castle}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * {{ill|Hachijuni Bank|en|Hachijuni Bank|WD=Q11390608|lt=Hachijūni Bank}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四柱神社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Taikyo Institute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}} on the {{ill|Japan Railways Group|en|Japan Railways Group|WD=Q864495|lt=JR|1=Japan Railways Group}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|WD=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto Castle|1=Matsumoto Castle}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * {{ill|Hachijuni Bank|en|Hachijuni Bank|WD=Q11390608|lt=Hachijūni Bank|1=Hachijuni Bank}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四柱神社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Ōte 3-3-20, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 14 | lat_seconds = 5.9 | long_degrees = 137 | long_minutes = 58 | long_seconds = 13.4 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|1=Kamimusubi}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} | type = Former Village Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | established = 1879 (Meiji 12) | festival = October 2 (Shintōsai) | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|14|5.9|N|137|58|13.4|E}}}} | label_position = bottom |caption=Haiden| location = {{ill|Ōte|WD=Q11435537|1=Ōte}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.234972|N|137.970389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yohashira Shrine''' (四柱神社, Yohashira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōte, Matsumoto|ja|大手 (松本市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōte, Matsumoto|1=Ōte|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435537}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shintō-san" due to the Shintō festival held in October. It is also commonly misread as "Shihashira." == Overview == [[File:Yohashira jinja01s1920.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine grounds]] The shrine's name derives from its enshrinement of four deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. {{nihongo|Hashira|柱|lit pillar}} is the counter for gods in Japanesehttps://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9F%B1 The shrine originated from the Shinto {{ill|Chūkyōin|ja|中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkyōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大教院|1=Taikyo Institute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435598}} established in Matsumoto, {{ill|Chikuma Prefecture|ja|筑摩県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Prefecture|1=Chikuma Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602831}}, in 1872 (Meiji 5). The Chūkyōin began enshrining the four deities in 1874 (Meiji 7). In 1879 (Meiji 12), the shrine was moved to its current location, and a shrine building was constructed, formally establishing Yohashira Shrine as a village shrine. The shrine building was destroyed in the Matsumoto Great Fire of 1888 (Meiji 21) and was rebuilt in 1924 (Taishō 13). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), Yohashira Shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine. == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}} on the {{ill|Japan Railways Group|en|Japan Railways Group|WD=Q864495|lt=JR|1=Japan Railways Group}} line * Alipico Transportation (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus), get off at Daimyōmachi bus stop via Matsumoto Castle, and it is immediately nearby == Nearby Points of Interest == * {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|WD=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto Castle|1=Matsumoto Castle}} * {{ill|Nawate Street|ja|縄手通り商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nawate Street|1=Nawate-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608106}} * {{ill|Hachijuni Bank|en|Hachijuni Bank|WD=Q11390608|lt=Hachijūni Bank|1=Hachijuni Bank}} Matsumoto Branch * {{ill|Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|ja|松本市時計博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|1=Matsumoto City Timepiece Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531031}} == See Also == * [[Toyama Chukyoin]], the Chukyoin of Nagano Prefecture == References == == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Yohashira-jinja}} * [http://www.go.tvm.ne.jp/~yohasira/ Official site of Yohashira Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|四柱神社|version=103952907|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四柱神社]] {{Commons category|Yohashira-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11420029}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
